title: Viral Whole Genome Sequencing Using Illumina Microbial Amplicon Prep (iMAP)
author: Stepfan de Villiers

steps.title: %s %s Make a dilution of the EPH3 as follows
steps.instruction: %s %s Make a dilution of the EPH3 as follows. The table indicated the required volume for 1 sample. Calculate the volumes required for the amount of samples included in the run. Reagent Volume (μL) per sample Required volume for x samples 1 EPH3 1.5 ... 2 Nuclease free water 3.0 ... 3 Total 4.5 ... EPH3 Dilution Mix The "Anneal RNA" step was modified to add a dilution of EPH3 instead of the default input. Previous testing has shown that reducing EPH3 input improves performance with amplicons larger than 400 bp. Illumina. 2023. Illumina Microbial Amplicon Prep for Viral Surveillance. Application Note M-GL-02215 v10. Illumina Inc.

steps.title: Reagent Storage Instructions 1 FSM (First...
steps.instruction: Reagent Storage Instructions 1 FSM (First Strand Mix) -25°C to -15°C Thaw at room temperature. Invert to mix and keep on ice. 2 RVT (Reverse Transcriptase) -25°C to -15°C Thaw at room temperature. Invert to mix and keep on ice. Overview of Reagents In a microcentrifuge tube, combine the following reagents to prepare the First Strand cDNA Master Mix : Reagent Volume (μL) per sample Required volume for x samples 1 FSM 4.5 ... 2 RVT 0.5 ... 3 Total 5.0 ... First Strand Master Mix

steps.title: Reagent Storage Instructions 1 Primer Pool...
steps.instruction: Reagent Storage Instructions 1 Primer Pool 1 -25°C to -15°C Thaw on ice. Vortex to mix and keep on ice. 2 Primer Pool 2 -25°C to -15°C Thaw on ice. Invert to mix and keep on ice. 3 IPM (Illumina PCR Mix) -25°C to -25°C Thaw on ice. Invert to mix and keep on ice. Overview of Reagents

steps.title: Post tagmentation clean up step
steps.instruction: Post tagmentation clean up step. Reagent Storage Instructions 1 ST2 (Stop Tagment Buffer 2) Room Temp Vortex before use. 2 TWB (Tagmentation Wash Buffer) 2°C to 8°C Invert to mix. Overview of Reagents

steps.title: Reagent Storage Instructions 1 EPM (Enriched...
steps.instruction: Reagent Storage Instructions 1 EPM (Enriched PCR Mix) -25°C to -15°C Invert to mix. Keep on ice until use. 2 Index Adapters -25°C to -15°C Thaw at room temperature. Vortex to mix and then centrifuge. Overview of Reagents

steps.title: Reagent Storage Instructions 1 IPB (Illumina...
steps.instruction: Reagent Storage Instructions 1 IPB (Illumina Purification Beads) Room temp Vortex thoroughly to mix. 2 RSB (Resuspension Buffer) 2°C to 8°C Bring to room temperature. Vortex and invert to mix. 3 Ethanol Room Temp Prepare a fresh solution of 80% EtOH Overview of Reagents

steps.title: All primers are ordered from IDT and resuspended in 0
steps.instruction: All primers are ordered from IDT and resuspended in 0.1× TE buffer to a final concentration of 100 micromolar (µM) each. Prepare multiple working primer pools to minimise freeze-thaw cycles. Primers are typically resuspended in 1x TE buffer according to manufacturer recommendations. But we routinely resuspend primers in 0.1x TE buffer to minimise potential inhibitory effects of EDTA on downstream PCR reactions while still providing adequate primer stability.

steps.title: Prepare the primer pool for the...
steps.instruction: Prepare the primer pool for the viral pathogens as described below at step 2. The primer scheme is described at step 1.1 for SARS-CoV-2, step 1.2 for RSV and step 1.3 for Dengue virus.

steps.title: SARS-CoV-2 Primers The SARS-CoV-2 primer scheme...
steps.instruction: SARS-CoV-2 Primers The SARS-CoV-2 primer scheme can be found at: ARTIC Primer Scheme for nCoV-2019 retrieved from: https://github.com/artic-network/primer-schemes/blob/3258a7fd11f39ad8b52f4743fe97aea4ec129544/nCoV-2019/V5.3.2/SARS-CoV-2.primer.bed A predesigned panel can be purchased from IDT: Artic V5.3.2 NCOV-2019 Panel – Catalog # 10016495 An overview of the primer sequences for SARS-CoV-2 is included at the bottom of the protocol.

steps.title: RSV Primers The primers used in...
steps.instruction: RSV Primers The primers used in this assay were designed by the CDC, which include 19 (RSVA) and 20(RSVB) amplicon pairs, targeting an amplicon size of ~925 bp and originally described by: Wang L, Ng TFF, Castro CJ, et al. Next-generation sequencing of human respiratory syncytial virus subgroups A and B genomes. J Virol Methods. 2022; 299:114335. doi:10.1016/j. jviromet.2021.114335. An overview of the primer sequences for RSV is included at the bottom of the protocol.

steps.title: Dengue Primers The Dengue virus primer...
steps.instruction: Dengue Primers The Dengue virus primer scheme can be found on the CERI CLIMADE consortium GitHub: https://github.com/CERI-KRISP/CLIMADE/blob/master/Protocols/Arboviruses/AllPrimerSchemes.xlsx An overview of the primer sequences for dengue virus is included at the bottom of the protocol.

steps.title: Primers are then assembled into two...
steps.instruction: Primers are then assembled into two pools to generate two overlapping sets of amplicons. For example, 5 µL of each 100 micromolar (µM) primer from a specific pool is transferred into a clean 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube and mixed thoroughly.

steps.title: Working 10 micromolar (µM) primer pools...
steps.instruction: Working 10 micromolar (µM) primer pools are subsequently prepared by combining 20 µL of the 100 micromolar (µM) primer pool with 180 µL of nuclease-free water (NFW).

steps.title: Add 4 µL EPH3 dilution to...
steps.instruction: Add 4 µL EPH3 dilution to each well of a new 96 wells PCR plate labelled cDNA . Add 4 µL RNA to each well.

steps.title: Seal, vortex ( 1600 rpm, °C,...
steps.instruction: Seal, vortex ( 1600 rpm, °C, 1 minute ), centrifuge ( 500 x g, °C, 1 minute ).

steps.title: Place on a thermal cycler and...
steps.instruction: Place on a thermal cycler and run the Anneal RNA program: Temperature (°C) Time 1 65 3 minutes 2 4 Hold Anneal RNA Program

steps.title: Add 4 µL master mix to...
steps.instruction: Add 4 µL master mix to each well of the cDNA plate.

steps.title: Seal, vortex ( 1600 rpm, °C,...
steps.instruction: Seal, vortex ( 1600 rpm, °C, 1 minute ), centrifuge ( 500 x g, °C, 1 minute ).

steps.title: Place on a thermal cycler and run the FSS Program
steps.instruction: Place on a thermal cycler and run the FSS Program. Temperature (°C) Time 1 25 5 minutes 2 42 30 minutes 3 80 5 minutes 4 4 Hold First Strand Synthesis Program Temperature and incubation times were modified as cDNA synthesis may be improved at lower temperatures (~42 °C). Safe Stopping Point - seal the plate and store at -25°C to -15°C

steps.title: Label two new PCR plates, PCR1 and PCR2
steps.instruction: Label two new PCR plates, PCR1 and PCR2 .

steps.title: In two microcentrifuge tubes, combine the...
steps.instruction: In two microcentrifuge tubes, combine the following reagents to prepare a PCR 1 Master Mix and a PCR 2 Master Mix. The indicated volumes are per sample. Reagent PCR 1 Master Mix (μL) PCR 2 Master Mix (μL) 1 IPM 7.5 7.5 2 Nuclease free water 2.3 2.3 3 PP1 (10 µM) 2.2 - 4 PP2 (10 µM) - 2.2 5 Total 12 12 PCR Master Mixes

steps.title: Add 10 µL PCR 1 Master...
steps.instruction: Add 10 µL PCR 1 Master Mix to each well of the PCR1 plate. Add 10 µL PCR 2 Master Mix to each well of the PCR2 plate. Add 2.5 µL from the cDNA plate to the corresponding wells of the PCR1 and PCR2 plates.

steps.title: Seal, vortex ( 1600 rpm, °C,...
steps.instruction: Seal, vortex ( 1600 rpm, °C, 1 minute ), centrifuge ( 500 x g, °C, 1 minute ).

steps.title: Place on a thermocycler and run...
steps.instruction: Place on a thermocycler and run the relevant PCR program depending on the pathogen of interest. Preheat Lid: 105 °C and Reaction Volume: 15 µL SARS-CoV-2 PCR program Temperature (°C) Time Cycle 1 98 3 minutes 2 98 15 seconds 35 cycles 3 64 5 minutes 4 4 Hold � RSV PCR %s %s The annealing temperature for the RSV PCR program was reduced to 56 °C to improve primer annealing. Dengue PCR %s %s

steps.title: At least 10% of the samples...
steps.instruction: At least 10% of the samples should be checked post-PCR using a Qubit Fluorometer and Qubit DNA HS Assay to ensure successful amplification. Safe Stopping Point – seal the plate and store at 2°C to 8°C or leave on the thermal cycler overnight.

steps.title: Label a new PCR plate TAG
steps.instruction: Label a new PCR plate TAG .

steps.title: Combine PCR1 and PCR2 as follows
steps.instruction: Combine PCR1 and PCR2 as follows: Transfer 5 µL from each well of the PCR1 plate to the corresponding wells of the TAG plate. Transfer 5 µL from each well of the PCR2 plate to the corresponding wells of the TAG plate.

steps.title: In a microcentrifuge tube, combine the...
steps.instruction: In a microcentrifuge tube, combine the following reagents to prepare the TagmentationMaster Mix. The indicated volume is per sample. %s %s

steps.title: Add 15 µL of the master...
steps.instruction: Add 15 µL of the master mix to each well of the TAG plate.

steps.title: Seal, vortex ( 1600 rpm, °C,...
steps.instruction: Seal, vortex ( 1600 rpm, °C, 1 minute ), centrifuge ( 500 x g, °C, 1 minute ).

steps.title: Place on a thermal cycler and run the TAG program
steps.instruction: Place on a thermal cycler and run the TAG program: Preheat Lid: 105 °C and Reaction Volume: 25 µL %s %s

steps.title: After the TAG program, immediately add...
steps.instruction: After the TAG program, immediately add 5 µL ST2 to each well of the TAG plate.

steps.title: Seal and shake
steps.instruction: Seal and shake.

steps.title: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Centrifuge briefly
steps.instruction: Centrifuge briefly.

steps.title: Place it on a magnetic stand...
steps.instruction: Place it on a magnetic stand and wait until the liquid is clear.

steps.title: Remove and discard the supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove and discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Wash beads as follows
steps.instruction: Wash beads as follows: a. Remove the plate from the magnetic stand. b. Add 50 µL TWB to each well.

steps.title: Seal, vortex ( 1600 rpm, °C,...
steps.instruction: Seal, vortex ( 1600 rpm, °C, 1 minute ), centrifuge ( 500 x g, °C, 1 minute ).

steps.title: Place it on the magnetic stand...
steps.instruction: Place it on the magnetic stand and wait until the liquid is clear.

steps.title: Remove and discard the supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove and discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Wash beads a second time
steps.instruction: Wash beads a second time . (repeat steps 7.7-7.9)

steps.title: Place it on the magnetic stand...
steps.instruction: Place it on the magnetic stand and wait until the liquid is clear. Leave supernatant in the plate after the second wash to prevent beads from overdrying.

steps.title: In a microcentrifuge tube, combine the...
steps.instruction: In a microcentrifuge tube, combine the following reagents to prepare the TAGPCR Master Mix : %s %s

steps.title: Vortex the TAG PCR Master Mix
steps.instruction: Vortex the TAG PCR Master Mix . With the plate on the magnetic stand, remove and discard the supernatant. Then use a P20 pipette to remove any remaining TWB .

steps.title: Remove the TAG plate from the magnetic stand
steps.instruction: Remove the TAG plate from the magnetic stand.

steps.title: Add 20 µL TAG PCR Master...
steps.instruction: Add 20 µL TAG PCR Master Mix to each well of the TAG plate. Add 5 µL Index Adapters to each well of the TAG plate.

steps.title: Seal, vortex ( 1600 rpm, °C,...
steps.instruction: Seal, vortex ( 1600 rpm, °C, 1 minute ), centrifuge ( 500 x g, °C, 1 minute ).

steps.title: Place on a thermocycler and run the TAG PCR Program
steps.instruction: Place on a thermocycler and run the TAG PCR Program. Preheat Lid: 100 °C and Reaction Volume: 25 µL Temperature (°C) Time Cycle 1 72 3 minutes 2 98 3 minutes 3 98 20 seconds 7 cycles* 4 60 30 seconds 5 72 1 minutes 6 72 3 minutes 7 10 Hold � TAG PCR Program Total cDNA Input (ng) Number of PCR cycles 1 1-24 12 2 25-99 9 3 100-500 7 To compensate for lower cDNA input amounts, the number of PCR cycles can be increased as above .

steps.title: Centrifuge the TAG plate at 500...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the TAG plate at 500 x g, °C, 1 minute .

steps.title: Place it on a magnetic stand...
steps.instruction: Place it on a magnetic stand and wait until the liquid is clear ( 00:03:00 ) .

steps.title: Label a new PCR plate IPB
steps.instruction: Label a new PCR plate IPB . Vortex the IPB tube to resuspend and add 22.5 µL of IPB to each well of the IPB plate.

steps.title: Transfer 25 µL supernatant from each...
steps.instruction: Transfer 25 µL supernatant from each well of the TAG plate to the corresponding wells of the IPB plate.

steps.title: Vortex to mix and centrifuge briefly
steps.instruction: Vortex to mix and centrifuge briefly.

steps.title: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Place it on a magnetic stand...
steps.instruction: Place it on a magnetic stand and wait for the liquid to clear ( 00:05:00 ) .

steps.title: Remove and discard the supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove and discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Wash beads as follows
steps.instruction: Wash beads as follows: a. Keep the plate on the magnetic stand and add 100 µL 80% EtOH to each well. b. Wait 00:00:30 , then remove and discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Wash the beads a second time
steps.instruction: Wash the beads a second time .

steps.title: Centrifuge the tube briefly to collect...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tube briefly to collect any remaining EtOH at the bottom of the plate.

steps.title: Place it on the magnetic stand...
steps.instruction: Place it on the magnetic stand and wait for the liquid to clear. Use a P20 pipette to remove ALL residual EtOH .

steps.title: Allow to air-dry on the magnetic stand for 00:01:00
steps.instruction: Allow to air-dry on the magnetic stand for 00:01:00 .

steps.title: Add 21 µL to resuspend the beads
steps.instruction: Add 21 µL to resuspend the beads.

steps.title: Vortex to mix and then centrifuge briefly
steps.instruction: Vortex to mix and then centrifuge briefly.

steps.title: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:02:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:02:00 .

steps.title: Place the tube on the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on the magnetic rack and wait for the liquid to clear ( 00:03:00 ) .

steps.title: Transfer 20 µL of supernatant to...
steps.instruction: Transfer 20 µL of supernatant to a new PCR plate labelled LIB . Safe Stopping Point – seal the plate and store at -25°C to -15°C

steps.title: Analyse 1 µL of each prepared...
steps.instruction: Analyse 1 µL of each prepared library using the Qubit dsDNA HS Assay on a Qubit fluorometer.

steps.title: Calculate the molarity of each prepared...
steps.instruction: Calculate the molarity of each prepared library ( use 350 bp as the average library size) .

steps.title: Dilute each library to a normalised...
steps.instruction: Dilute each library to a normalised concentration of 4 nanomolar (nM) using RSB .

steps.title: Pool 5 µL from each library into a clean 1
steps.instruction: Pool 5 µL from each library into a clean 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Quantify the pool by qPCR using...
steps.instruction: Quantify the pool by qPCR using the Takara Library Quantification Kit .

steps.title: [Optional] Run 1 µL of the...
steps.instruction: [Optional] Run 1 µL of the library pool on a TapeStation using the D1000 ScreenTape assay to confirm the average library size.

steps.title: Using the qPCR quantification, dilute the...
steps.instruction: Using the qPCR quantification, dilute the library to the starting concentration for your chosen sequencing platform/kit.

steps.title: Follow the denature and dilute instructions...
steps.instruction: Follow the denature and dilute instructions for your chosen sequencing platform by referring to the manufacturer's user guides. Sequencing Platform/Kit Starting Concentration (nM) Final Loading Concentration (pM) 1 NextSeq (Standard-SBS) 2 750 2 NextSeq (XLEAP-SBS) 2 650 3 MiSeq i100 2 80 4 MiSeq V2 4 10-12 5 MiSeq V3 4 12-14 Loading Concentration Recommendations by Platform/ Kit
